data.title: Trader Eugene: Market views need to be adjusted flexibly, being overly bearish may be the biggest mistake
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Well-known trader Eugene said that he is constantly re-evaluating the market's bearish stance, because the real big money is often earned when it is long, and over-insisting on bearishness may be the biggest mistake.
Eugene emphasized that market views need to be flexible, and new information may change the market pattern at any time. The key is to quickly interpret and adjust strategies. He reminded investors not to be overconfident in any market forecasts, but to make corresponding judgments based on real-time data.
As previously reported, Eugene pointed out that Bitcoin (BTC) still has downside risks and may test MicroStrategy founder Michael Saylor's psychological level of $66,000. He believes that the upward trend or range of BTC has been destroyed in all time frames, and $75,000 is the last support. In addition, considering the correlation between BTC and US stocks, he does not expect a reversal in the short term due to the remarks of Trump, Bessent or Federal Reserve Chairman Powell.

data.title: Trading Strategy Shift Leads To Losses For Noted Trader
data.description:
According to PANews, the renowned trader known as 'Swing Whale,' famous for achieving an 81% success rate in swing trading, has recently altered his trading strategy. Previously known for stable swing trading, the trader has shifted to high-frequency trading, frequently switching between long and short positions within a single day. However, this change in approach has not resulted in profits and has instead led to underperformance.

As of now, 'Swing Whale' appears to have paused trading activities. On March 4, he invested 58.38 million USDT to purchase 679 WBTC at an average price of $85,964 and spent 8.8 million USDT to acquire 4,212 ETH at an average price of $2,090. Currently, these positions are experiencing an unrealized loss of approximately $2.74 million.

data.title: Trader Eugene Reevaluates Bearish Market Stance Amid Changing Dynamics
data.description:
According to Odaily, renowned trader Eugene is continuously reassessing his bearish market position, emphasizing the importance of flexibility in market perspectives. He notes that substantial profits are often made when going long, and excessive adherence to bearish views could be a significant mistake. Eugene highlights the necessity of swiftly interpreting new information and adjusting strategies accordingly. He advises investors to avoid overconfidence in any market predictions and to make judgments based on real-time data.

Previously, Eugene pointed out that Bitcoin (BTC) still faces downside risks and might test the psychological threshold of $66,000 set by MicroStrategy founder Michael Saylor. He believes that BTC's upward trend or range has been disrupted across all time frames, with $75,000 being the final support level. Considering BTC's correlation with U.S. stocks, Eugene anticipates no short-term reversal due to statements from U.S. President Donald Trump, Bessent, or Federal Reserve Chairman Powell.

data.title: NFT series One Gravity completes FCFS phase casting, floor price rises to 1.42 ETH
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the NFT series One Gravity has completed the FCFS stage of casting on the Ethereum chain; the casting price of each NFT is 0.1 ETH, the current floor price is 1.42ETH, and the 24-hour trading volume has reached 350 ETH, ranking second in Opensea.

It is reported that One Gravity is the first NFT series built for the AI ​​Layer 1 ecosystem 0G. Adhering to the concept of "community first", the series has a total of 1,888 NFTs. Through the One Gravity NFT series, 0G users will have the opportunity to become co-builders of the community. In the FCFS stage of the One Gravity series, one address can purchase 2 NFTs. The project party temporarily adjusted the contract before casting, limiting the transaction volume submitted each time to 1, so that real community users can purchase at least 1.

data.title: "Whales" recently suffered high-frequency trading failures, with a floating loss of $2.74 million on BTC and ETH holdings of $67.18 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Ember's monitoring, the "band whale" previously known for its 81% swing trading win rate has recently undergone a dramatic change in trading style, from steady swing trading to high-frequency long-short switching, but this strategy did not bring the expected returns, but instead fell into losses.
As of now, the whale seems to have suspended operations. On March 4, he spent 58.38 million USDT to buy 679 WBTC at an average price of $85,964, and spent 8.8 million USDT to buy 4,212 ETH at an average price of $2,090. However, this part of the position currently has a floating loss of about $2.74 million.

data.title: Trader Eugene: Bitcoin still has room to go down, or test the key psychological level of $66,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Well-known trader Eugene said that the overall trend of Bitcoin (BTC) is still biased downward, and there has not been enough market panic on the high time frame (HTF) to complete the deep cleansing of this cycle.
Eugene believes that although BTC has some support around $76,000 and may even form a double bottom in the $74,000-76,000 area, the probability is gradually decreasing. He pointed out that the upward trend or range of Bitcoin has been broken in all time frames, and $75,000 is the last important support.
In addition, he believes that the market will sooner or later test the psychological level of $66,000 in the mind of MicroStrategy founder Michael Saylor and observe the market's performance at this level. At the same time, due to the strong correlation between BTC and US stocks, he does not expect a reversal in the short term due to the remarks of Trump, Bessent or Fed Chairman Powell.
Eugene also said that if the US PPI data performs well tonight and pushes Bitcoin to rebound to a high of more than $80,000, he might consider shorting at high levels.
